resolved_to_corpus_ids.NO Za 1a: (no NO prefix in corpus — Nerokourou Za 1 not ingested)
resolved_to_corpus_ids.ZA Za 3: ZA Zb 3 (catalog letter difference: Za vs Zb)

gorila_refs_not_in_corpus.note: 6 of 7 apparent mismatches resolve to corpus rows via suffix/catalog normalization. The outlier 'NO Za 1a' is a Nerokourou inscription not present in corpus.json — flag for ingestion.

corpus_docs_absent_from_gorila_A_301_ind.explanation: The 45 HT Wa entries are roundel and nodule sealings from Haghia Triada that GORILA catalogues in its sealings corpus (Volume 2), not in the Volume 5 sign index proper. Their absence from the sign index is expected: the sign index is a syllabogram-in-word concordance, and the HT Wa materials use A 301 as a standalone logogram (one sign per sealing). The GORILA sealings volume gives their attestation list.

notes: The 72 ↔ 35 count difference is not an inconsistency. GORILA indexes different document types in different volumes. Sign-index rows are the concordance for syllabogram use; sealings/nodules are concorded separately. This cross-check confirms that where both indices overlap (running-text occurrences), coverage is complete after sub-face normalization, with the single exception of NO Za 1a (Nerokourou) which should be added to corpus.json.
